#### 8. I18N SEO Rules

##### 8.1 Architecture

- EN = root (zodiacid.com/*)
- RU = subdirectory (zodiacid.com/ru/*)
- ET = subdirectory (zodiacid.com/et/*)
- Same filenames across all languages
- Each language is a complete, standalone set of HTML files

##### 8.2 Per-Language Content Rules

- ALL visible text in page language — zero mixing
- RU/ET content is written natively, NOT translated from English
- Brand name "ZodiacID" stays in Latin everywhere
- Prices always in EUR with "EUR" prefix (not $ or the euro sign in different formats)

##### 8.3 Per-Language Schema Rules

- Every JSON-LD block must have `"inLanguage": "{lang}"` where applicable
- All text fields (name, description, question, answer) in page language
- URLs point to the language-specific page
- `sameAs` Wikidata links stay identical (language-neutral)
- BreadcrumbList: translate `name` values, use localized URLs

##### 8.4 Per-Language Meta Rules

- `<html lang="{lang}">` matches content language
- `<title>` in page language
- `<meta name="description">` in page language
- All OG title/description in page language
- `og:locale` matches: `en_US`, `ru_RU`, `et_EE`
- `og:url` points to the localized URL

#### 9. SEO Validation Checklist

##### Pre-Launch (every new page or language version)

**Structure:**
- [ ] Unique `<title>` (no duplicates across entire site including all languages)
- [ ] Unique `<meta name="description">`
- [ ] Self-referencing `<link rel="canonical">`
- [ ] `<meta name="robots">` present
- [ ] Correct `<html lang="">` attribute
- [ ] Semantic H1 > H2 > H3 hierarchy, one H1 per page

**Hreflang:**
- [ ] All 4 hreflang tags present (en, ru, et, x-default)
- [ ] Tags are bidirectional across all 3 language versions
- [ ] x-default points to EN root version
- [ ] URLs in hreflang match actual file paths

**Schema:**
- [ ] JSON-LD validates in Google Rich Results Test
- [ ] Product schema has all fields (price, availability, returnPolicy, deliveryLeadTime)
- [ ] Article schema has datePublished, inLanguage, author, publisher with logo
- [ ] FAQPage schema matches visible FAQ content exactly
- [ ] `inLanguage` present on all content schemas
- [ ] No schema on 404.html

**Open Graph:**
- [ ] og:title, og:description in page language
- [ ] og:image with absolute URL
- [ ] og:url matches canonical
- [ ] og:site_name = "ZodiacID"
- [ ] og:locale matches page language
- [ ] Test: paste URL in Telegram — correct language title/description shown

**Technical:**
- [ ] sitemap.xml includes all public pages with hreflang
- [ ] robots.txt allows crawling of all public pages
- [ ] No mixed content (HTTP on HTTPS)
- [ ] 404.html returns 404 status code (not 200)
- [ ] All images have `alt`, `width`, `height`
- [ ] No orphan pages (every page reachable via internal link)
- [ ] Core Web Vitals pass (PageSpeed Insights)

**I18N:**
- [ ] All internal links within RU pages point to /ru/ paths
- [ ] All internal links within ET pages point to /et/ paths
- [ ] EN internal links use root paths (no prefix)
- [ ] Language switcher links to correct equivalent page
- [ ] No English text visible on RU/ET pages (except brand name)
- [ ] Product prices match across all languages
- [ ] Disclaimer in footer translated for each language

##### Post-Launch (weekly for 4 weeks)

- [ ] Google Search Console: no crawl errors
- [ ] Google Search Console: pages indexed vs submitted per language
- [ ] Google Search Console: no manual actions
- [ ] Hreflang audit (Screaming Frog or Ahrefs)

##### Monthly

- [ ] Schema validation after content updates
- [ ] Dead link check
- [ ] Verify RU/ET pages indexed (`site:zodiacid.com/ru/`, `site:zodiacid.com/et/`)
- [ ] `priceValidUntil` in Product schema not expired

### Risks

1. Bad translations = worse than no translation. Native speakers must QA.
2. Celebrity birth time disputes — some celebs have unverified times.
3. Russian language on EU site may attract sanctioned-jurisdiction traffic. Payment processor handles geo-blocking.
4. 3x maintenance burden — every content change needs 3 files updated.
5. Celebrity samples could be seen as endorsement — use "sample analysis" framing.

### Kill Criteria

1. EN landing page isn't live yet -> ship EN first. (Currently live — proceed.)
2. No validated birth data for 2+ celebrities in any language -> reduce that language's scope.
3. Total site size exceeds CF Pages free tier limits.

lines: +1. **CRITICAL: Broken navigation on 6 RU/ET celebrity sample pages** — all nav/breadcrumb/CTA/footer links used `href="ru/..."` / `href="et/..."` from within the subdirectory, creating double-prefix 404 paths (e.g., `/ru/ru/index.html`). Fixed: stripped prefix, all links now relative.
+2. **Missing canonical + x-default on 6 RU/ET celebrity sample pages** — added self-referencing canonical and x-default hreflang.

+3. **Incorrect hreflang on 4 EN sample pages** (beyonce, lennon-ono, prince-george, pitt-jolie) — pointed to non-existent `/ru/` and `/et/` equivalents. Fixed: removed invalid hreflang, language switcher now links to `/ru/sample/` and `/et/sample/` hub pages.
+4. **English skip-link on 6 RU/ET sample pages** — "Skip to content" → "Перейти к содержанию" (RU) / "Mine sisuni" (ET).
+5. **English aria-labels on 6 RU/ET sample pages** — localized all nav, toggle, switcher, breadcrumb, report-toc labels.
+6. **English footer location on 6 RU/ET sample pages** — "Tallinn, Estonia" → "Таллинн, Эстония" (RU) / "Tallinn, Eesti" (ET).

+Note on hreflang for celebrity samples: since celebrities are language-specific (not translations), each page only has hreflang for its own language + x-default. This is correct per Google's hreflang spec — hreflang should only reference actual translations.
+All other checks PASSED: html lang, robots meta, titles/descriptions, OG tags, schema.org, sitemap, robots.txt, _headers, internal link isolation.

Phase 3 is essentially the same validation checklist as Phase 2, but more detailed. All the issues Phase 2 found are already fixed. Phase 3 ends with **"СТОП. Ревью человеком."** — that's the gate for you to review before deploy.

## Summary of Phase 2 (Стресс-тест) — DONE

**Found & fixed 6 issues across 10 HTML files:**

| # | Issue | Files | Severity |
|---|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | **Broken navigation** — double-prefix paths (`/ru/ru/...`) | 6 RU/ET sample pages | CRITICAL |
| 2 | Missing canonical + x-default hreflang | 6 RU/ET sample pages | High |
| 3 | Incorrect hreflang to non-existent pages | 4 EN sample pages (beyonce, lennon-ono, prince-george, pitt-jolie) | High |
| 4 | English "Skip to content" in localized pages | 6 RU/ET sample pages | Low |
| 5 | English aria-labels in localized pages | 6 RU/ET sample pages | Low |
| 6 | English footer location text | 6 RU/ET sample pages | Low |

**Everything else passed:** `lang` attrs, robots meta, titles, OG tags, schema.org (Product, FAQPage, Article, ContactPage, ItemList), sitemap (51 URLs), `_headers`, `robots.txt`, internal link isolation, no English text leaking into content.

**Next:** Phase 3 (Sweep) is the same checklist for human review, followed by Phase 4 (deploy). Want me to proceed with Phase 3, or do you want to review the changes first?
